metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend (or replace) the `_module_level_imported_names` helper in `test_backend_neutrality.py` with an `ast.walk`-based scan so the new AST gate actually catches function-nested `adapters.jira`/`acli_subprocess` imports — the four removal sites (fetcher.py:379, fetcher.py:441, applier.py:603, _attestation.py:65) are all function-nested, so the current `tree.body`-only approach cannot gate them.
evidence: Plan AC1: 'fetcher.py, applier.py, apply_handlers.py, _attestation.py contain NO adapters.jira/acli_subprocess import (module-level or function-nested) — verified by the new AST case'
test_backend_neutrality.py _module_level_imported_names walks only tree.body: 'for node in tree.body: if isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom)' — this excludes function-nested imports by construction
fetcher.py:379: 'from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira import acli_subprocess' is inside a function body (not module-level)
fetcher.py:441: 'from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira import acli_subprocess' is inside a try-block inside a function
applier.py:603: 'from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira import acli_subprocess' is inside a function body
_attestation.py:65: 'from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira import acli_subprocess' is inside a function body
The plan says the gate catches both module-level AND function-nested; the existing helper only catches module-level; the plan does not describe extending the helper
findings.finding: The plan asserts the new AST case in test_backend_neutrality.py will catch 'module-level or function-nested' adapters.jira imports, but the existing _module_level_imported_names helper only walks tree.body (top-level AST), and ALL FOUR of the vendor-import sites being removed (fetcher.py:379/441, applier.py:603, _attestation.py:65) are function-nested lazy imports — so the existing helper infrastructure cannot catch a regression in the function-nested positions the gate is supposed to pin.
findings.location: AC1: 'NO adapters.jira/acli_subprocess import (module-level or function-nested) — verified by the new AST case in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py'
scenarios: If an implementer adds the new AST test case using the existing _module_level_imported_names helper (the natural reuse), a future re-introduction of a function-nested 'from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira import acli_subprocess' in any of the four files will silently pass CI — exactly the regression the gate is supposed to prevent.
The plan's own AC says function-nested violations are in scope for the gate, but the described testing approach does not specify the full-AST walk (ast.walk) needed to cover them.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the body of the new gate test with an ast.walk scan: `all_from_imports = [n for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, ast.ImportFrom)]`, then check that no node's module string contains 'adapters.jira' or 'acli_subprocess'. This covers both module-level and function-nested positions as AC1 requires.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exception class that `assert_env_ready()` raises in the Backend Protocol (e.g. add a neutral `BackendEnvError` to `_backend.py` analogous to `BackendAssigneeNotFoundError`, or explicitly state it raises `EnvironmentError`/`ValueError`) so the port contract is fully specified.
evidence: "assert_env_ready() — raises listing missing connection essentials"
"assert_env_ready() raises naming exactly the missing JIRA_* vars" (Testing section)

The Backend Protocol `_backend.py` scope bullet defines BackendAssigneeNotFoundError as a named neutral exception, but no equivalent named neutral exception is defined for assert_env_ready() failures.
ABSENCE: no exception class name for assert_env_ready()'s raise path appears anywhere in the plan.
findings.finding: The Backend Protocol's `assert_env_ready()` method has no specified raised-exception contract in the port definition. The plan says it 'raises listing missing connection essentials' and the Testing section says it 'raises naming exactly the missing JIRA_* vars', but neither the Scope section nor the AC names the exception class that `assert_env_ready()` should raise (e.g. a new neutral `BackendEnvError`, `EnvironmentError`, `ValueError`). Without a named exception type, the Protocol definition is under-specified for type-checking and for consumers of the port who might need to catch it.
findings.location: Approach section — 'assert_env_ready()' bullet; Acceptance Criteria AC2
scenarios: An executor writing the Protocol stub must choose an exception type; two implementors may choose differently, breaking callers that catch it.
make typecheck may flag assert_env_ready()'s raise as untyped if the Protocol's raises contract is left implicit.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a neutral `BackendEnvError(RuntimeError)` to `_backend.py` (mirroring the BackendAssigneeNotFoundError pattern already planned), annotate `assert_env_ready()` in the Protocol as `-> None` raising `BackendEnvError`, and have `JiraBackend.assert_env_ready()` raise `BackendEnvError` wrapping the current _attestation.py message. Update AC2 to reference `BackendEnvError` explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit expand-contract note (or Scope entry) identifying all existing Backend implementors beyond JiraBackend (e.g., test stubs/mocks) that must also grow the three new members, and confirm the update sequence or provide a rollback path if typecheck fails.
evidence: Scope lists only JiraBackend as implementing the new members; no other implementors are mentioned.
AC3 requires 'make typecheck' green, which will catch broken implementors at CI time, but no rollback path or expand-contract sequence for those implementors is described.
Existing test_backend_neutrality.py already exercises Backend implementors and likely contains a stub that would need updating.
findings.finding: The Backend Protocol extension adds three new required members (project, query_project, assert_env_ready) and a new exception, but the plan does not acknowledge or provide expand-contract sequencing for any Backend implementors beyond JiraBackend (e.g., test stubs, a MockBackend, or a future GitHubBackend) that would be broken by the structural-typing contract change.
findings.location: Approach section ('Extend the Backend Protocol…') and Scope ('_backend.py (port: project/query_project/assert_env_ready + BackendAssigneeNotFoundError)')
scenarios: An implementer updates _backend.py and JiraBackend but is surprised to find typecheck failures in test stubs or other Backend implementations not listed in Scope, with no guidance on how to handle them.
A future GitHubBackend that was Protocol-compliant before this change now fails typecheck with no migration path described.
findings.suggested_fix: In Scope or Approach, add: 'Any test-double or other Backend implementor (list them) must also implement project/query_project/assert_env_ready — add stub/raise-NotImplementedError bodies as part of this ticket so typecheck remains green without requiring downstream tickets.' If no other implementors exist, state that explicitly to close the ambiguity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add ADR 0035 (or AGENTS.md / design-doc) update to Scope and AC, documenting the four new Backend Protocol members (`project`, `query_project`, `assert_env_ready()`, `BackendAssigneeNotFoundError`) as required contract obligations for all backend implementors.
evidence: Plan references 'ADR 0035 §(d)' as governing but lists no ADR/doc update in Scope
Scope lists five .py files — no ADR or AGENTS.md entry
AC2 specifies 'Backend port exposes project, query_project, assert_env_ready(), and BackendAssigneeNotFoundError' with no mention of documenting them in the ADR
A future GitHub backend author has no canonical doc explaining they must implement assert_env_ready() or subclass BackendAssigneeNotFoundError
findings.finding: The plan extends the Backend Protocol with four new members (`project`, `query_project`, `assert_env_ready()`, `BackendAssigneeNotFoundError`) that every future backend implementor must satisfy, but no update to ADR 0035 or AGENTS.md is listed in Scope or Acceptance Criteria. The governing ADR currently does not document these new required contract members, leaving future implementors (e.g., a GitHub backend) without a canonical reference for what the port now mandates.
findings.location: Scope section; Acceptance Criteria AC2; ADR 0035 §(d) (referenced but not listed for update)
scenarios: A developer adding a third backend reads ADR 0035 and sees the old port surface; they omit assert_env_ready() or BackendAssigneeNotFoundError, causing runtime failures discovered only at integration time.
A plan-review or onboarding audit finds the ADR and the code out of sync, increasing maintenance cost.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to Scope: `docs/adr/0035-backend-port.md (update: document project, query_project, assert_env_ready(), BackendAssigneeNotFoundError as required Backend Protocol members)` and add an AC: '`ADR 0035` updated to list all new port members and their semantics.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `inbound_probe.py` to the Scope, rewire its `_resolve_env()` through `Backend.assert_env_ready()` (or at minimum document it as a conscious out-of-scope residual with rationale), and extend the AC1 AST gate to cover it.
evidence: inbound_probe.py:65: `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira import acli_subprocess`
inbound_probe.py:67: `settings = acli_subprocess.resolve_jira_settings()` followed by the same JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN fail-fast check the plan moves into `assert_env_ready()`
Plan Scope lists only: fetcher.py, applier.py, apply_handlers.py, _attestation.py — inbound_probe.py is absent
Plan AC1 gate covers only 'fetcher.py, applier.py, apply_handlers.py, _attestation.py' — inbound_probe.py would NOT be covered by the new AST assertion
findings.finding: inbound_probe.py is an unenumerated consumer of the exact same `resolve_jira_settings()` pattern targeted by this ticket but is absent from the Scope, Testing, and AC.
scenarios: After this ticket lands, inbound_probe.py still imports from adapters.jira directly (env-ready check) while _attestation.py no longer does — the invariant is partial and inbound_probe becomes a new debt item.
If the neutrality gate is later broadened to cover inbound_probe.py, it would fail immediately because this ticket did not rewire it.
A future non-Jira backend would need a parallel `_resolve_env()` in inbound_probe.py — the exact problem the plan solves for _attestation.py.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add inbound_probe.py to the scope list and rewire `_resolve_env()` to call `backend.assert_env_ready()` via the registry seam (same pattern as _attestation.py), extending the AC AST gate to the five modules; or (b) add an explicit 'Documented residual' note explaining why inbound_probe.py is excluded (e.g., it is loaded by-path via spec_from_file_location and cannot safely import from the registry seam at load time).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in the Approach section explaining why `project`/`query_project`/`assert_env_ready()` are placed on the required `Backend` facade (vs. an opt-in `BackendProjectScope` capability Protocol), specifically addressing how non-project-scoped backends should satisfy the interface.
evidence: Plan says 'Extend the Backend Protocol … with scalar, read-only members' but gives no justification for why these belong on the required Backend facade vs. an opt-in capability Protocol (the pattern already used for SupportsLinks/SupportsComments/SupportsIncremental in _backend.py:154-186).

Current _backend.py defines optional capabilities as @runtime_checkable sub-Protocols precisely for vendor-specific behaviours; project-scoping is similarly vendor-specific (a GitHub backend may not have a project key at all).
ABSENCE: no statement in the plan of why flat scalar properties are preferred over a BackendProjectScope capability protocol.
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for placing `project`, `query_project`, and `assert_env_ready()` directly on the `Backend` facade Protocol rather than behind a sub-protocol (e.g. a `BackendProjectScope` capability). Without this, an implementer has no guidance on whether future backends that are project-less (e.g. a flat-list GitHub backend) should raise `NotImplementedError` or return empty strings from `project`/`query_project` — a design question the plan leaves silent.
findings.location: Approach section — 'Extend the Backend Protocol … with scalar, read-only members'
scenarios: A future GitHub/Linear backend implementer sees `project: str` as a required Backend member and must implement it with a no-op stub, violating the ADR 0035 §(d) principle the plan otherwise carefully follows.

An implementer who inlines project-less backends as empty-string shims will silently disable the fetcher's fail-closed guard (`query_project` empty → jql_active raises), breaking the safety contract for non-Jira backends.

findings.finding: The plan adds `diff_fields`, `extract_field`, `assignee_matches`, and `local_type_to_remote` to the vendor-neutral `OutboundMapper` Protocol, but these are Jira-specific field-comparison helpers that take Jira payload shapes as parameters (`jira_fields: dict` in `_diff_fields`, `_extract_jira_field`). The established Port-and-Adapter pattern (Cockburn) places field-comparison/diffing logic in the neutral core (which already owns diff/apply per ADR 0035 §(d).2), using the port only for vendor↔canonical translation (`map_local_to_remote`). Widening `OutboundMapper` with helpers parameterized on vendor field shapes contradicts the neutrality goal: a future GitHub adapter would be forced to implement `extract_field(jira_fields, ...)` with Jira-named semantics, re-coupling the port to Jira.

The Approach section names `JiraBackend.assert_env_ready()` as the home of the JIRA_* fail-fast but lists Backend port additions as only `project` and `query_project` read-only accessors — `assert_env_ready()` is absent from the port additions list. Yet the same Approach sentence routes `_attestation` 'through the port', which requires `assert_env_ready()` to be a port-level method. This creates a contradiction: if `assert_env_ready()` is only on the concrete `JiraBackend`, `_attestation.py` cannot call it through the port without importing `JiraBackend` from `adapters.jira`, violating AC1.
findings.location: Approach section — 'Backend gains project (DIG-defaulted, applier guard) + query_project (un-defaulted, fetcher fail-closed JQL) read-only accessors' vs 'JiraBackend.assert_env_ready() carries the bootstrap JIRA_* fail-fast. Route ... _attestation through the port'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach section, update 'Backend gains ...' to include `assert_env_ready()` alongside `project` and `query_project`, and note that JiraBackend's implementation validates JIRA_* env vars there.
